title: apicra imbricata
intro: Apicra imbricata, also known as the Mexican prickly poppy, is a plant species native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including relieving pain, reducing fever, and treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Apicra imbricata can be prepared into herbal remedies, including infusions, tinctures, and salves, which are often used to treat skin conditions like wounds, burns, and eczema.
